name: Configuration & Data Models
description: Loads and validates ledger.yaml configuration. Defines all shared Pydantic models used across the system: SchemaFile, Field, Backend, Annotation, ClassificationTier (fixed enum: PUBLIC/PII/FINANCIAL/AUTH/COMPLIANCE), MigrationGate, MigrationPlan, ChangelogEntry, PropagationRule. Provides the annotation propagation table — a data-driven dict mapping each built-in annotation to its propagation targets (Pact assertion type, Arbiter tier behavior, Baton masking rule, Sentinel severity). Custom annotations from ledger.yaml extend this table at load time. Also defines the CONFLICTS pairs (immutable+gdpr_erasable, audit_field+gdpr_erasable, soft_delete_marker+immutable) and REQUIRES rules as data. Provides file-locking utility (fcntl-based) for changelog and plans directory writes.

types.name: ClassificationTier
types.description: Fixed enumeration of data classification tiers. Implemented as @final StrEnum in Python. No runtime extension permitted.

types.name: PropagationRule

fields.name: annotation_name
fields.description: The canonical annotation name this rule applies to.

fields.name: pact_assertion_type
fields.description: The Pact assertion type generated for this annotation (e.g. 'field_present', 'type_match', 'not_null').

fields.name: arbiter_tier_behavior
fields.description: How Arbiter should treat fields with this annotation (e.g. 'enforce_tier', 'audit_only', 'block_downgrade').

fields.name: baton_masking_rule
fields.description: The Baton masking rule to apply (e.g. 'full_mask', 'partial_mask', 'no_mask', 'hash').

fields.name: sentinel_severity
fields.description: The Sentinel alert severity for violations of this annotation (e.g. 'critical', 'high', 'medium', 'low', 'info').

validators.error_message: sentinel_severity must be one of: critical, high, medium, low, info

types.description: Defines how a single annotation propagates to each downstream system. Frozen Pydantic model.

types.name: Annotation

fields.name: name
fields.description: The canonical annotation name (must exist in the propagation table).

fields.name: params
fields.description: Optional key-value parameters for the annotation.

types.description: A single annotation applied to a field, referencing a known annotation name and optional parameters. Frozen Pydantic model.

types.name: Field

functions.name: load_config
functions.description: Reads and validates ledger.yaml from the given path. Parses via yaml.safe_load, validates into Pydantic models, merges custom annotations into the propagation table with collision detection, preserves raw YAML for all schema files, and returns a composed LedgerConfig. On validation failure, raises LedgerValidationError with all violations and file path context. Never crashes on a single error — always aggregates.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Filesystem path to the ledger.yaml configuration file.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: The file at path does not exist

error_data.path: The path that was not found

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: The file at path is not readable

error_data.path: The path that could not be read

error_cases.name: invalid_yaml
error_cases.condition: The file content is not valid YAML

error_cases.name: validation_errors
error_cases.condition: The YAML content fails Pydantic model validation (missing required fields, type mismatches, constraint violations)

error_cases.name: annotation_collision
error_cases.condition: A custom annotation name collides with a builtin annotation name

error_cases.name: constraint_violations
error_cases.condition: One or more fields have annotation sets that violate CONFLICTS or REQUIRES rules

error_data.file_path: Path to the schema file with violations

preconditions: path points to an existing, readable file
File contents are valid YAML (parseable by yaml.safe_load)
postconditions: Returned LedgerConfig.propagation_table contains all builtin annotations plus any valid custom annotations from ledger.yaml
All SchemaFile.raw_yaml fields contain the exact bytes read from disk, not round-tripped YAML
No builtin annotation name has been overwritten by a custom annotation
All CONFLICTS and REQUIRES constraints have been validated for every field's annotation set across all loaded schemas
side_effects: Reads schema YAML files referenced in configuration from disk

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the main configuration file using yaml.safe_load

structured_side_effects.target: Schema YAML files referenced in ledger.yaml schemas_dir
structured_side_effects.description: Reads each schema YAML file to parse fields and preserve raw_yaml content

performance_budget.complexity: O(S * F) where S = number of schemas, F = max fields per schema

functions.name: build_propagation_table
functions.description: Merges custom annotation definitions with the builtin propagation table. Detects and rejects name collisions between custom and builtin annotations. Returns an immutable MappingProxyType[str, PropagationRule] containing all annotations.

inputs.name: custom_annotations
inputs.description: List of custom annotation definitions to merge. May be empty.

error_cases.name: name_collision
error_cases.condition: One or more custom annotation names match a builtin annotation name

error_cases.name: duplicate_custom_names
error_cases.condition: Two or more custom annotations share the same name

error_data.duplicates: List of duplicate custom annotation names

preconditions: All custom annotations have non-empty, valid snake_case names
All custom annotations have valid sentinel_severity values
postconditions: Returned dict (MappingProxyType at runtime) contains all builtin annotations unchanged
Returned dict contains all provided custom annotations that do not collide with builtins
Returned dict is immutable (MappingProxyType) and cannot be modified by callers
No builtin annotation entry has been modified or removed

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects. Constructs and returns new immutable mapping.

performance_budget.complexity: O(B + C) where B = number of builtins, C = number of custom annotations

functions.name: validate_annotation_set
functions.description: Validates a set of annotation names against the CONFLICTS and REQUIRES constraint data. Returns a complete list of all violations found — never short-circuits on the first error. An empty return list indicates no violations.

inputs.name: annotations
inputs.description: The set of annotation names to validate. Order does not matter; duplicates are ignored.

error_cases.name: empty_annotation_name
error_cases.condition: One or more annotation names in the input set are empty strings

preconditions: All annotation names in the set are non-empty strings
postconditions: Every CONFLICTS pair where both members are present in the input set produces exactly one 'conflict' violation
Every REQUIRES rule where the source annotation is present but one or more required annotations are missing produces exactly one 'missing_required' violation per missing annotation
The returned list is exhaustive — all violations are reported, not just the first

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function. Checks annotations against static CONFLICTS and REQUIRES data.

performance_budget.complexity: O(A * C + A * R) where A = number of annotations, C = number of CONFLICTS pairs, R = number of REQUIRES rules

functions.name: get_builtin_propagation_table
functions.description: Returns a read-only copy of the builtin propagation table (without custom annotations). Useful for introspection and testing. The returned value is a MappingProxyType[str, PropagationRule].
postconditions: Returned dict is a MappingProxyType and cannot be mutated
Contains exactly the builtin annotation set, no custom annotations

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function returning a reference to the module-level immutable builtin table.

functions.name: get_conflicts
functions.description: Returns the CONFLICTS constant: a frozenset of frozenset[str] pairs representing mutually exclusive annotation combinations. Exactly three pairs: {immutable, gdpr_erasable}, {audit_field, gdpr_erasable}, {soft_delete_marker, immutable}.
postconditions: Returned value is a frozenset containing exactly 3 frozenset[str] pairs
Returned value is identical to the module-level CONFLICTS constant

structured_side_effects.description: Pure accessor returning the immutable module-level CONFLICTS constant.

functions.name: get_requires
functions.description: Returns the REQUIRES constant: a dict mapping annotation names to frozensets of annotation names that must co-occur. Immutable at module level.
postconditions: Returned value is the module-level REQUIRES dict
Keys are annotation names, values are frozenset[str] of required co-annotations

structured_side_effects.description: Pure accessor returning the immutable module-level REQUIRES constant.

functions.name: file_lock
functions.description: Context manager that acquires an advisory file lock (fcntl.flock) on a .lock sidecar file adjacent to the given path. Supports exclusive (write) and shared (read) locks. Creates the .lock file if it does not exist. On non-Unix platforms, raises PlatformError. Used for coordinating concurrent writes to changelog and migration plans directories.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Filesystem path of the file or directory to protect. The lock is placed on path + '.lock'.

inputs.name: exclusive
inputs.description: If true, acquire an exclusive (LOCK_EX) lock. If false, acquire a shared (LOCK_SH) lock.

inputs.name: blocking
inputs.description: If true, block until the lock is available. If false, raise immediately on contention.

error_cases.name: platform_unsupported
error_cases.condition: Running on a non-Unix platform (e.g. Windows) where fcntl is not available

error_data.message: fcntl-based file locking is only supported on Unix platforms

error_cases.name: lock_contention
error_cases.condition: blocking=false and the lock is already held by another process

error_data.path: The lock file path that could not be acquired

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: Cannot create or open the .lock sidecar file due to filesystem permissions

error_cases.name: parent_dir_missing
error_cases.condition: The parent directory of path does not exist

preconditions: Running on a Unix platform with fcntl support
The parent directory of path exists and is writable (for .lock sidecar creation)
postconditions: On context entry: the .lock sidecar file exists and is locked (exclusive or shared) per the requested mode
On context exit: the lock is released and the file descriptor is closed
The original file at path is never modified by the locking mechanism itself
side_effects: Creates .lock sidecar file if it does not exist
Acquires advisory fcntl.flock lock on the sidecar file

structured_side_effects.target: .lock sidecar file adjacent to the given path
structured_side_effects.description: Creates the .lock sidecar file if it does not exist, opens it, and acquires an fcntl.flock advisory lock.
Acquires and later releases an fcntl advisory lock, affecting OS-level lock tables.

functions.name: parse_schema_file
functions.description: Parses a single schema YAML file into a SchemaFile model. Reads the file, preserves raw YAML verbatim, parses via yaml.safe_load, validates into Field/Annotation models, and runs CONFLICTS/REQUIRES validation on every field's annotation set. Returns all violations on failure, not just the first.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Filesystem path to the schema YAML file.

validators.error_message: Schema file path must be non-empty

inputs.name: propagation_table
inputs.description: The active propagation table (builtin + custom) for validating that all annotation names in the schema are known.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: The schema file does not exist

error_data.path: The path that was not found

error_cases.name: invalid_yaml
error_cases.condition: The file content is not valid YAML

error_cases.name: schema_validation_errors
error_cases.condition: The parsed YAML fails SchemaFile/Field/Annotation Pydantic validation

error_cases.name: unknown_annotation
error_cases.condition: A field references an annotation name not present in the propagation_table

error_data.violations: List of unknown annotation names with field context

error_cases.name: constraint_violations
error_cases.condition: One or more fields have annotation sets violating CONFLICTS or REQUIRES

error_data.violations: All constraint violation messages with field context

preconditions: path points to an existing, readable YAML file
propagation_table contains all valid annotation names (builtin + custom)
postconditions: Returned SchemaFile.raw_yaml is the exact string content read from disk
All field annotation names exist in the propagation_table
All field annotation sets pass CONFLICTS and REQUIRES validation
SchemaFile.source_path equals the input path
side_effects: Reads schema YAML file from disk

structured_side_effects.target: Schema YAML file at the given path
structured_side_effects.description: Reads the schema file content as raw string and parses via yaml.safe_load

performance_budget.complexity: O(F * A) where F = number of fields, A = max annotations per field

invariants: ClassificationTier enum is fixed at exactly five members: PUBLIC, PII, FINANCIAL, AUTH, COMPLIANCE. No runtime additions or removals are permitted.

CONFLICTS is an immutable frozenset of exactly three frozenset pairs: {immutable, gdpr_erasable}, {audit_field, gdpr_erasable}, {soft_delete_marker, immutable}. It is never modified after module load.
REQUIRES is an immutable dict mapping annotation names to frozensets of required co-annotations. It is never modified after module load.
The builtin propagation table is immutable after module load. Custom annotations may extend the table at config load time but may never overwrite or shadow a builtin annotation name.
The public propagation table returned by build_propagation_table is a MappingProxyType and cannot be mutated by callers.
Schema YAML content stored in SchemaFile.raw_yaml is the exact byte-for-byte string read from disk — never round-tripped through pyyaml serialization, reformatted, normalized, or reordered.
All validation functions return complete lists of all violations found, never short-circuiting on the first error.
All Pydantic models that represent domain data use ConfigDict(frozen=True) for immutability.
LedgerConfig is the sole composed return type of load_config and contains the merged propagation table, validated schemas, and resolved configuration.
file_lock operates only on Unix platforms with fcntl support; a clear PlatformError is raised on unsupported platforms.

data_access.rationale: Reads ledger.yaml configuration files which may reference schema definitions containing PII, FINANCIAL, AUTH, and COMPLIANCE classified fields. The loader parses these references to build propagation tables and validate annotation sets. Writes are limited to INTERNAL-tier .lock sidecar files created during file-locking operations for changelog and migration plan directory access. Raw YAML content (which may describe PII/FINANCIAL/AUTH/COMPLIANCE fields) is stored verbatim in SchemaFile.raw_yaml but is never transformed or normalized.

side_effects.rationale: Reads ledger.yaml configuration and referenced schema YAML files from disk via yaml.safe_load to construct LedgerConfig and SchemaFile models.
Creates and manages .lock sidecar files adjacent to changelog and plans directories using fcntl.flock for exclusive/shared file access coordination.
Acquires fcntl.flock advisory locks on sidecar .lock files to coordinate concurrent writes to changelog and migration plans directories.

authority.rationale: This component is the single authoritative source for the ledger.yaml configuration schema, the fixed ClassificationTier enum (PUBLIC/PII/FINANCIAL/AUTH/COMPLIANCE), the builtin annotation propagation table mapping annotations to Pact/Arbiter/Baton/Sentinel behaviors, and the CONFLICTS/REQUIRES constraint data. All other components depend on these definitions; no other component may redefine or override them.
